rulings.Case Title: BRUCE GLEASON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Mannington Mills, Inc.¿s motion to apply Arizona law: hearing required. The motion appears to be based on the assumption that the determination of what law applies to a claim for a defective product and the issue of apportionment of liability (i.e. joint/several liability) is to be made on a defendant by defendant basis, yet no authority is cited in support of that assumption. If weither side has located such authority, be prepared to discuss such authority at the hearing. If one or both of the issues cannot be decided on a defendant by defendant basis, the moving party appears to have provided insufficient evidence to permit an informed determination of what state law should apply to that issue or issues. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether defendant¿s employees exposed plaintiff to asbestos-containing pipe insulation. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are sustained only as to the sentence in paragraph 7 stating ¿no one at my deposition . . . underground pipe,¿ as plaintiff was asked if he saw defendant perform any hands-on work at the airport. (Plaintiff¿s deposition, 1362: 17-19.) The rest of plaintiff¿s statements in paragraph 7 are not inconsistent with his deposition testimony. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant Triple A Machine Shop, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. The court issues no tentative ruling on the negligence claim, but tentatively determines that defendant is entitled to adjudication in its favor on the claims for strict liability and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)
